file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1173.lean` — Erdős Problem 1173

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` (line 17).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–29).
- [x] Starts with heading: `# Erdős Problem 1173`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1173`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description: "A problem of Erdős and Hajnal."
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Ko25b]` (Koepke) and `[Va99]` (Vaughan).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1173 ... end Erdos1173` (lines 33–80).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1173`.
- [x] `open Ordinal Cardinal` appears **before** the namespace block (line 31).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `GCH` (line 37) has a LaTeX docstring describing the Generalized Continuum Hypothesis.
- [x] `omegaOmega1` (line 40) has a docstring and is marked `noncomputable` (uses `.ord`).
- [x] `IsFreeSet` (lines 45–47) has a docstring explaining the free-set condition.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `GCH`, `IsFreeSet`.
- [x] Value-level definition uses `lowerCamelCase`: `omegaOmega1`.

No local notation or `@[category test/API]` sanity checks are present, but none are needed here.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1173` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 3 5]` (line 68).
- [x] Theorem has a docstring (lines 49–67) referencing `[Ko25b, Problem 35]` and `[Va99, 7.88]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks: *"Does there exist a free set of cardinality $\aleph_{\omega+1}$?"* — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` pattern (line 69).
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (existence of a large free set under GCH and the given set-mapping conditions), so `answer(True)` would mean "yes."
- [x] All quantifiers (`∀ (h : GCH) (f : ...) ...`) appear **after** `answer(sorry) ↔`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category research open]`.
- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
- [x] Theorem is `research open` and its proof body contains `sorry`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 3 5]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 3, 5).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order: 3 < 5.
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] Both 3 (Mathematical logic and foundations) and 5 (Combinatorics) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` with `sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry`, lines 77–78). Acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A — no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `⊆`, `≤`, `<`, `↔`, `ω`, `ℕ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter passes not verified in this review.

---

**Verdict:** All checkable items pass. The formalization is clean, well-documented, and fully conformant with the style guidelines. The `answer()` usage correctly follows Case 1 (yes/no question) with all quantifiers on the RHS. The three custom definitions (`GCH`, `omegaOmega1`, `IsFreeSet`) are appropriately docstrung and correctly marked for computability. AMS codes 3 and 5 are reasonable for a set-theoretic combinatorics problem involving GCH.

content.text: The review passed — all checkable items have checkmarks, and the verdict says "All checkable items pass." The only unchecked items are build verification (items 12), which are noted as "not verified in this review" rather than failures. No changes needed.
